bullet_points: Load the dataset and validate formula strings.
Parse formulas to extract metal elements (all elements except O and C) and identify oxide-carbonate reaction pairs where the metal set matches exactly.
Retain the most stable polymorph (lowest energy_above_hull) for each unique chemical system.
Print summary statistics: total oxide/carbonate entries, unique chemical systems, and valid pairs found per metal family (Ca, Mg, Li, Na, K, Ba, Sr, Mn, Fe, Co, Ni, Cu, Zn, Al).
Save the processed pairs to a file.
Calculate the 0 K reaction energy ΔE = E(MxCO3) - E(MxOy) - E(CO2) on a per-formula-unit basis, using the reference energy for CO2 (-11.17 eV/formula unit).
Apply a strict filter requiring both the oxide precursor and the carbonate product to have an energy_above_hull <= 0.1 eV/atom.
Print the top 20 most favorable and top 20 least favorable pairs by reaction energy.
Save the calculated reaction energies and filtered candidate list.
Compute ΔG(T, P) = ΔH - TΔS for each pair, using a fixed approximation for CO2 entropy loss (ΔSCO2 ≈ -0.214 kJ/mol/K).
Perform sensitivity analysis (500 K to 1200 K at PCO2 = 0.15 bar) to identify equilibrium temperature (Teq) where ΔG ≈ 0.
Generate a multi-panel figure: (a) histogram of ΔE by metal family, (b) scatter plot of ΔE vs Teq colored by metal family, and (c) box plots of ΔG at 700 K grouped by metal family.
Save the figure and the temperature-dependent analysis results.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Gibbs Free Energy, Equilibrium Analysis, and Visualization

bullet_points: Estimate melting point proxy: Tmelt_proxy = |formation_energy_per_atom| * density * scaling_constant.
Calibrate the scaling constant using known oxides (CaO, MgO) and print the calibration check results.
Filter out candidates with Tmelt_proxy < 800 K.
Perform impurity screening to exclude materials prone to forming low-melting-point sulfates.
Save the filtered candidate list.
Filter candidates based on band_gap (> 0.5 eV) and is_metal (False).
Rank final candidates using a weighted composite score (balancing ΔG at 700 K, Tmelt_proxy, and band gap).
Print a final ranked table of the top 20 candidates with all relevant scores.
Save the final ranked table as a CSV file.
sub_tasks.sub_task: Electronic Property Filtering and Final Ranking

bullet_points: Write a complete Results section structured as: (1) Stoichiometric Pairing Statistics, (2) Thermodynamic Reaction Energy Landscape, (3) Gibbs Free Energy and Equilibrium Temperature Analysis, (4) Thermal Stability and Electronic Property Filtering, and (5) Final Candidate Ranking.
Reference specific numerical results, statistics, and plot descriptions generated in previous steps to support the analysis.
Discuss the industrial viability of the identified optimal-delta candidates.
